items.title: Terrence Deacon - Philosophy of Evolutionary Developmental Biology [Evo-Devo]
items.author: Closer To Truth
items.content:
<article>Subscribe to the Closer To Truth podcast on Apple, Spotify, or wherever you get your podcasts: https://shorturl.at/mtJP4

How does evolutionary developmental biology reshape our deepest philosophical questions? Explore the emerging field of Evo Devo which connects evolution and development to reveal how complex life forms arise. 

Follow Closer To Truth on Instagram for updates, announcements, and videos: https://shorturl.at/EzYo9

Terrence William Deacon is an American neuroanthropologist. He taught at Harvard for eight years, relocated to Boston University in 1992, and is currently Professor of Anthropology and member of the Cognitive Science Faculty at the University of California, Berkeley.

items.title: Liad Mudrik - Consciousness Studies: History and Future
items.author: Closer To Truth
items.content:
<article>Sign up for a free Closer To Truth membership to receive exclusives like early access to new episodes: https://closertotruth.com/register/

What is the history of consciousness studies—during the past century and especially over the last 35 or so years? What advances have been made? What challenges remain? What problems seem intractable? What are trends and expectations for the future?

Make a tax-deductible donation of any amount to help support Closer To Truth continue making content like this: https://shorturl.at/OnyRq

Prof. Liad Mudrik received two PhD degrees in cognitive neuroscience (TAU & HUJI) and in philosophy (TAU); the latter was awarded with the Robert J. Glushko prize for outstanding dissertations in cognitive science. She is a researcher at the school of psychological sciences and Sagol school of neuroscience at Tel Aviv University. Her research focuses on conscious experience, its neural mechanisms and functions.

items.title: Keith Ward - Does God Intervene in Human Affairs?
items.author: Closer To Truth
items.content:
<article>Like us on Facebook for daily videos, updates, announcements, and much more: https://shorturl.at/tak4l

Is God, if there is a God, the ‘God of History’? Does God make specific interventions in the course of human events to ensure that God’s purpose be fulfilled, for nations as well as for individuals? Or did God so set the laws of nature before the foundation of the world such that God’s goals would be achieved without God’s special, active interventions?

Donate to help Closer To Truth continue exploring the world's deepest questions without the need for paywalls: https://closertotruth.com/donate/

Keith Ward FBA is an English philosopher and theologian. He is a fellow of the British Academy and a priest of the Church of England. He was a canon of Christ Church, Oxford, until 2003. Comparative theology and the relationship between science and religion are two of his main topics of interest.

items.title: Nathan Lents - Philosophy of Sex, Sexuality, & Gender
items.author: Closer To Truth
items.content:
<article>Follow us on X for videos, articles, and polls on life's biggest questions: https://x.com/CloserToTruth

Sexual questions abound. What is “normal” sexual behavior vs. multiple variations? How to appreciate LGBTQ+ issues? What can be learned from sex in animal models that is relevant for human sexuality? A philosophical perspective can provide clarity.

Sign up for a free Closer To Truth membership to receive exclusives like early access to new episodes: https://closertotruth.com/register/

Nathan H. Lents is an American scientist, author, and university professor. He has been on the faculty of John Jay College since 2006 and is currently the director of the Cell and Molecular Biology program and the former head of the honors program and the campus Macaulay Honors College program.

items.title: Galen Strawson - Theories of Consciousness
items.author: Closer To Truth
items.content:
<article>Support the show with a Closer To Truth merch purchase. Shop T-shirts, mugs, notebooks, hoodies, and more: https://bit.ly/3P2ogje

Explore diverse theories of consciousness including materialism, dualism, panpsychism, idealism, and diverse ways of thinking about consciousness from the perspectives of neuroscience, philosophy, wisdom traditions. Compare and contrast diverse theories of consciousness as they are arrayed on a “Landscape of Consciousness”.

Make a donation of any amount to help Closer To Truth continue exploring the world's deepest questions: https://closertotruth.com/donate/

Galen John Strawson is a British analytic philosopher and literary critic who works primarily on philosophy of mind, metaphysics, John Locke, David Hume, Immanuel Kant and Friedrich Nietzsche.

items.title: Philip Goff - Panpsychism: Arguing Pro and Con
items.author: Closer To Truth
items.content:
<article>Follow us on X for videos, articles, and polls on life's biggest questions: https://x.com/CloserToTruth

Panpsychism is the theory that consciousness is irreducible and exists fundamentally at the foundations of reality. Panpsychism forms include ‘micropsychism,’ where fundamental particles or fields are in some sense conscious, and ‘Cosmopsychism,’ where the entire universe is in some sense conscious. What are the arguments for and against Panpsychism like the ‘combination problem’?

Sign up for a free Closer To Truth membership to receive exclusives like early access to new episodes: https://closertotruth.com/register/

Philip Goff is a British author, panpsychist philosopher, and professor at Durham University whose research focuses on philosophy of mind and consciousness. Goff is also the co-host of the podcast Mind Chat. He blogs on Substack, and his work has been published in The Guardian, Aeon and Scientific American, among others.

Subscribe to the Closer To Truth podcast on Apple, Spotify, or wherever you listen: https://shorturl.at/mtJP4

items.title: William Grassie - Fallacies in Arguing for God
items.author: Closer To Truth
items.content:
<article>Closer To Truth is now on Bluesky! Follow us for updates, announcements, and more: https://shorturl.at/GSR4k

Believers in God should be appalled when poor arguments are offered to ‘prove’ the existence of God. These arguments can be so bad that one might think the proponents are really atheists in disguise. It gives God no credit to defend God’s existence with faulty information or fallacious arguments. 

Like us on Facebook for daily videos, updates, announcements, and much more: https://shorturl.at/tak4l

William John Grassie is an American interdisciplinary scholar, social activist, and organizational consultant. He received his doctorate in comparative religion from Temple University and his BA in political science from Middlebury College.

items.title: Silvia Jonas - Is Mathematics Eternal?
items.author: Closer To Truth
items.content:
<article>Make a donation to help Closer To Truth continue exploring the world's deepest questions without the need for paywalls: https://closertotruth.com/donate/

Mathematics is like nothing else. The truths of math seem to be unrelated to anything else—independent of human beings, independent of the universe. The sum of 2 + 3 = 5 cannot not be true; this means that 3 + 2 = 5 would be true even if there were never any human beings, even if there were never a universe! When then, deeply, is mathematics?

Follow Closer To Truth on Instagram for daily videos, updates, and announcements: https://www.instagram.com/closertotruth/

Silvia Jonas is a Professor of Philosophy at the University of Bamberg, an external member of the Munich Center for Mathematical Philosophy, and an associated member of the Konstanz Center for Philosophy of Mathematics. She also serves on the board of trustees of the Martin Buber Society of Fellows and is a trusted advisor for the Ernst Ludwig Ehrlich Studienwerk.

items.title: Peter Godfrey-Smith - Philosophy of Evolutionary Culture & Social Behavior
items.author: Closer To Truth
items.content:
<article>Subscribe to the Closer To Truth podcast on Apple, Spotify, or wherever you get your podcasts: https://shorturl.at/mtJP4

Can an evolutionary perspective help explain culture and various social behaviors? Is it proper to use “culture” for non-human animals? If so, what is “animal culture” and how is human culture distinguished?

Follow Closer To Truth on Instagram for updates, announcements, and videos: https://shorturl.at/EzYo9

Peter Godfrey-Smith is an Australian philosopher of science and writer, who is currently Professor of History and Philosophy of Science at the University of Sydney.

items.title: Dean Radin on the State of Psi Studies | Closer To Truth Chats
items.author: Closer To Truth
items.content:
<article>Dean Radin surveys the current landscape of psi studies — key findings, ongoing debates, and what the scientific community still struggles to understand about anomalous mental phenomena.

Radin's book, "The Science of Magic: How the Mind Weaves the Fabric of Reality", is on sale now: https://amzn.to/3KXvPd7

Watch more CTT Chats here: https://t.ly/jJI7e

Dean Radin is an American parapsychologist. Following a bachelor and master's degree in electrical engineering and a PhD in educational psychology Radin worked at Bell Labs, as a researcher at Princeton University and the University of Edinburgh, and was a faculty member at University of Nevada, Las Vegas.</article>

items.title: Walter Sinnott-Armstrong - Do Angels and Demons Exist?
items.author: Closer To Truth
items.content:
<article>Sign up for a free Closer To Truth membership to receive exclusives like early access to new episodes: https://closertotruth.com/register/

Many theologians take angels and demons seriously. Why? Certainly, most human beings believe in angels and demons. Anecdotal cases are legion. Certainly, such nonphysical beings, in one form or another, populate most of the world’s religions. But angels and demons seem out of place, anachronistic, in our modern world. Is there anything here we should take seriously?

Make a tax-deductible donation of any amount to help support Closer To Truth continue making content like this: https://shorturl.at/OnyRq

Alexander Rosenberg is an American philosopher and novelist. He is the R. Taylor Cole Professor of Philosophy at Duke University, well known for contributions to philosophy of biology and philosophy of economics. Rosenberg describes himself as a "naturalist".

items.title: Bernardo Kastrup - Idealism: Arguing Pro and Con?
items.author: Closer To Truth
items.content:
<article>Like us on Facebook for daily videos, updates, announcements, and much more: https://shorturl.at/tak4l

Idealism is the theory that all reality is consciousness and only consciousness, and that what we perceive to be the physical world is either a manifestation of consciousness or derived from it. Either way, fundamental reality is pure consciousness. What are the arguments for and against Idealism (like the common sense of the physical)?

Donate to help Closer To Truth continue exploring the world's deepest questions without the need for paywalls: https://closertotruth.com/donate/

Bernardo Kastrup is a Dutch philosopher and computer engineer whose work centers on consciousness studies and analytic idealism, a form of metaphysical idealism developed within the analytic tradition. He questions physicalism and argues that consciousness lies at the foundation of reality.

Wear your support for the show with a Closer To Truth merchandise purchase: https://bit.ly/3P2ogje

items.title: John Dupré - Philosophy of Evolutionary Biology
items.author: Closer To Truth
items.content:
<article>Contribute what you can to help Closer To Truth continue exploring the world's deepest questions without the need for paywalls: https://closertotruth.com/donate/

Explore evolution—its scope and depth. How did life on Earth come to be as it is, and how did humans come to be as we are? What are the deep principles driving evolution? What are evolution’s challenges and open questions?

Follow Closer To Truth on Instagram for daily videos, updates, and announcements: https://www.instagram.com/closertotruth/

John A. Dupré is a British philosopher of science. He is the director of Egenis, the Centre for the Study of Life Sciences, and professor of philosophy at the University of Exeter. Dupré's chief work area lies in philosophy of biology, philosophy of the social sciences, and general philosophy of science.
